title: Windows: Podman without admin rights cannot create \\.\pipe\docker_engine for Docker API
body:
### Issue Description

I want to use Podman under Windows, without admin rights.

As stated in a previous [Issue #17232](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/17232#issuecomment-1405571778), this should be possible. (WSL is installed beforehand).

However, when executing as a normal user, an error message appears that API forwarding failed to start:
````
API forwarding for Docker API clients is not available due to the following startup failures.
        CreateFile \\.\pipe\docker_engine: Das System kann die angegebene Datei nicht finden.
````

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install podman (4.6.2 podman-remote-release-windows_amd64.zip)
2. Initialise podman machine ``podman machine init``
3. Start podman machine ``podman machine start``


### Describe the results you received

When executing in elevated PowerShell:

```
PS > podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"

This machine is currently configured in rootless mode. If your containers
require root permissions (e.g. ports < 1024), or if you run into compatibility
issues with non-podman clients, you can switch using the following command:

        podman machine set --rootful

API forwarding listening on: npipe:////./pipe/docker_engine

Docker API clients default to this address. You do not need to set DOCKER_HOST.
Machine "podman-machine-default" started successfully
```

When executing as a normal user

````
PS > podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"

This machine is currently configured in rootless mode. If your containers
require root permissions (e.g. ports < 1024), or if you run into compatibility
issues with non-podman clients, you can switch using the following command:

        podman machine set --rootful

API forwarding for Docker API clients is not available due to the following startup failures.
        CreateFile \\.\pipe\docker_engine: Das System kann die angegebene Datei nicht finden.

Podman clients are still able to connect.
Machine "podman-machine-default" started successfully
````



### Describe the results you expected

Docker API can also be used when using podman without admin rights.

### podman info output

comments.body:
I did some digging myself, but beware, I am not really firm with Windows programming.

It seems that podman machine uses ``win-sshproxy.exe`` for the named pipe: [function launchWinProxy](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/18561f26adf94a97ffeec9fb7f6de9ec4ccaea57/pkg/machine/wsl/machine.go#L1260C14-L1260C14)

The ssh proxy seems to be the [gvisor-tap-vsock](https://github.com/containers/gvisor-tap-vsock/tree/f93fe269e82f603d130df62ab862ee59b4ecce9a) repo.

There, some Windows magic is happening with [access rights](https://github.com/containers/gvisor-tap-vsock/blob/f93fe269e82f603d130df62ab862ee59b4ecce9a/pkg/sshclient/npipe_windows.go#L17C33-L17C60)
```
const SddlDevObjSysAllAdmAll = "D:P(A;;GA;;;SY)(A;;GA;;;BA)"
```

From what I gathered, [finally ](https://github.com/microsoft/go-winio/blob/e6aebd619a7278545b11188a0e21babea6b94182/pipe.go#L376C8-L376C29 )the Windows API function ``ntCreateNamedPipeFile`` is called.

The [documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-hardware/drivers/kernel/sddl-for-device-objects) states:
```
SDDL_DEVOBJ_SYS_ALL_ADM_ALL

"D:P(A;;GA;;;SY)(A;;GA;;;BA)"

SDDL_DEVOBJ_SYS_ALL_ADM_ALL allows the kernel, system, and administrator complete control over the device. No other users may access the device.
```

So it would makes sense, that as a normal user a pipe with these access rights cannot be created?

However, I don't now why those parameters where chosen and if other parameters would work as well.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@n1hility thanks for taking a look. It seems you're right, the named pipe seems to be made eventually. I had to run the command a few times until it popped up.

```PS C:\Windows\system32> podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"

This machine is currently configured in rootless mode. If your containers
require root permissions (e.g. ports < 1024), or if you run into compatibility
issues with non-podman clients, you can switch using the following command:

        podman machine set --rootful

API forwarding for Docker API clients is not available due to the following startup failures.
        CreateFile \\.\pipe\docker_engine: Das System kann die angegebene Datei nicht finden.

Podman clients are still able to connect.
Machine "podman-machine-default" started successfully
PS C:\Windows\system32> dir \\.\pipe\ | Where-Object Name -Match docker_engine
PS C:\Windows\system32> dir \\.\pipe\ | Where-Object Name -Match docker_engine
PS C:\Windows\system32> dir \\.\pipe\ | Where-Object Name -Match docker_engine
PS C:\Windows\system32> dir \\.\pipe\ | Where-Object Name -Match docker_engine


    Verzeichnis: \\.\pipe


Mode                 LastWriteTime         Length Name
----                 -------------         ------ ----
------        01.01.1601     01:00              2 docker_engine

PS C:\Windows\system32> $Env:DOCKER_HOST = "npipe:////./pipe/docker_engine"
PS C:\Windows\system32> docker ps
CONTAINER ID   IMAGE     COMMAND   CREATED   STATUS    PORTS     NAMES
PS C:\Windows\system32> podman machine stop
Machine "podman-machine-default" stopped successfully
PS C:\Windows\system32> docker ps
error during connect: this error may indicate that the docker daemon is not running: Get "http://
%2F%2F.%2Fpipe%2Fdocker_engine/v1.24/containers/json": open //./pipe/docker_engine: Das System ka
nn die angegebene Datei nicht finden.```

@n1hility unfortunately I cannot reproduce any more. Not sure what changed, but my machine is also remotely managed, so I don't know what (e.g. Windows) updates were pushed.

Even without admin rights, the script runs in approx. 45 ms.

```
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"

This machine is currently configured in rootless mode. If your containers
require root permissions (e.g. ports < 1024), or if you run into compatibility
issues with non-podman clients, you can switch using the following command:

        podman machine set --rootful

API forwarding listening on: npipe:////./pipe/docker_engine

Docker API clients default to this address. You do not need to set DOCKER_HOST.
Machine "podman-machine-default" started successfully
Waiting for docker_engine...
Found docker_engine!


Days              : 0
Hours             : 0
Minutes           : 0
Seconds           : 0
Milliseconds      : 46
Ticks             : 460324
TotalDays         : 5.32782407407407E-07
TotalHours        : 1.27867777777778E-05
TotalMinutes      : 0.000767206666666667
TotalSeconds      : 0.0460324
TotalMilliseconds : 46.0324

Machine "podman-machine-default" stopped successfully
```
Could this be a result of a check the anti virus software does and delays the creation of the named pipe? (If anti virus software even checks pipes...)

How to proceed? Close this ticket?
I will keep an eye out and will reopen this ticket (or create a new one?) if it happens again, starting with providing the timing you requested?

@n1hility I think this is a problem specific to my machine. Today the problem does appear again, so I can provide you with a measurement.

I want to point out, that after the printing of ``podman machine set --rootful`` it already takes some time to continue. This was not the case two days ago. So I guess there is already some kind of blocking method waiting for the pipe to be created.

```
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"

This machine is currently configured in rootless mode. If your containers
require root permissions (e.g. ports < 1024), or if you run into compatibility
issues with non-podman clients, you can switch using the following command:

        podman machine set --rootful

API forwarding for Docker API clients is not available due to the following startup failures.
        CreateFile \\.\pipe\docker_engine: Das System kann die angegebene Datei nicht finden.

Podman clients are still able to connect.
Machine "podman-machine-default" started successfully
Waiting for docker_engine...
Found docker_engine!


Days              : 0
Hours             : 0
Minutes           : 0
Seconds           : 21
Milliseconds      : 680
Ticks             : 216802527
TotalDays         : 0.000250928850694444
TotalHours        : 0.00602229241666667
TotalMinutes      : 0.361337545
TotalSeconds      : 21.6802527
TotalMilliseconds : 21680.2527

Machine "podman-machine-default" stopped successfully
```

Thanks @MastermindPanda this is helpful. That does seem like a significant delay. I agree that this is likely system specific. My best guess would be your system is actively being scanned by some virus/malware detection agent, is undergoing updates, or is being monopolized by some other system activity. However, I will look into whether the handling could be a little more robust or at least better informing of the problem. I will close this particular issue out for now since we ruled out a permission bug, but thank you for opening it and sharing all these details.
